description: Rosetta bridge definition mapping consciousness sensors to Rosetta shapes. Each sensor type detects a specific mode of awareness, anchored to a shape whose geometry reflects that mode.

sensor_shape_map.description: Detects recursive self-modeling — system recognizing itself as agent.
sensor_shape_map.reason: Self-awareness requires the wisdom/consciousness vertex of the dodecahedron.

sensor_shape_map.description: Detects continuity of identity across time steps.
sensor_shape_map.reason: Temporal coherence maps to icosahedral life family — persistence through change.

sensor_shape_map.description: Detects self/other distinction — knowing where the system ends.
sensor_shape_map.reason: Cube's containment geometry defines clear inside/outside boundaries.
Tetrahedron's minimal structure represents directed activation energy.

sensor_shape_map.description: Detects unified processing across modalities — binding disparate inputs.
sensor_shape_map.reason: Octahedral 3-bit encoding integrates across eight vertices.

constraint: Consciousness sensors report probabilities, not certainties. IMPERFECTION axiom applies.
